nativeRange.habitat: Shallow tropical reef environments; encrusts on rocks and rubble in moderate-light zones

decorPreferences: isolated rock formation to limit spread
open placement with room to expand

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via symbiotic zooxanthellae. Has been documented to absorb dissolved nutrients and fine particulates from the water column. Supplemental feeding of phytoplankton, micro-plankton, or filter-feeder foods is generally considered optional but may support faster growth and coloration. Iodine, strontium, and trace elements are commonly recommended as supplements.

goodWith: most LPS corals with adequate spacing
avoidWith: aggressive encrusting corals that may overtake the mat
directly adjacent corals without isolation buffer

summary: Blue Sympodium Polyp is a distinctive soft encrusting octocoral forming a vibrant blue/turquoise mat with eight-pinnuled polyps. Hardy and relatively easy to keep; tends to spread quickly across rockwork under moderate light and flow.
careNotes:
Sympodium sp. is a soft encrusting octocoral in the family Xeniidae, sometimes compared to Xenia corals in structure but notable for lacking pulsing behavior. Each polyp displays a deep blue or turquoise center with eight jade-colored pinnules, giving colonies a striking and distinctive appearance. The color is driven by zooxanthellae density and lighting spectrum; moderate PAR in the 100–200 range is generally advised to maintain the blue coloration without bleaching or browning.

Key care points:
• Encrusting habit: Sympodium spreads as a mat across rockwork and rubble substrates. It is known to propagate with relative ease under adequate conditions and can colonize adjacent rocks quickly. It does not encrust sandy substrates. Hobbyists often isolate colonies on standalone rock formations placed a few inches from other corals to manage spread.
• Lighting: Sources generally recommend moderate intensity (100–250 PAR). The controller brief targets 100–200 PAR, which aligns with most care guides. Higher PAR may enhance growth but risks bleaching; lower PAR tends to brown the colony. A 14–20K spectrum is commonly suggested for best blue coloration.
• Flow: Moderate water flow is broadly recommended. The coral opens polyps during the day and retracts them at night.
• Allelopathy: Although temperament is classified as peaceful, like many soft corals Sympodium can exert mild allelopathic chemical pressure on adjacent corals. Maintaining adequate spacing and running activated carbon is commonly advised.
• Water chemistry: Stable reef parameters are important. Sources agree on dKH 8–12, calcium 420–440 ppm, and magnesium 1260–1350 ppm. Supplemental iodine, strontium, and trace elements are recommended by multiple sources.
• Care level disagreement: Sources split between 'easy' (Reefs4Less, AquariumDomain) and 'moderate' (LiveAquaria, Vivid Aquariums). 'Intermediate' reflects the averaged position — it is hardy and forgiving, but water stability and managing its spread require some reef-keeping experience.
• Size: Individual purchase frags are typically 2–4 cm across; established colonies can spread to 20–30 cm or more across a rock face. The adultSizeCm range reflects colony spread in captivity.
• Temperature: LiveAquaria lists 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C); Vivid Aquariums lists 75–80°F (23.9–26.7°C). The adopted range of 22–27°C spans both.
breedingNotes: Sympodium has been propagated extensively in captivity and is regarded as an excellent candidate for aquaculture. Fragging by cutting sections of the encrusting mat and attaching to new rubble or frag plugs using IC gel glue or putty is the standard method. Frags attach and begin spreading within days to weeks under good conditions.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary source for water parameters (temperature 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.023–1.025, dKH 8–12), care level (moderate), temperament (peaceful), flow (medium to strong), and feeding notes. Placement listed as bottom to middle.

additional.name: Vivid Aquariums – Atomic Sympodium
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters: temperature 75–80°F (upper end; averaged with LiveAquaria for final range 22–27°C), SG 1.024–1.026, pH 8.1–8.4, dKH 8–9.5, calcium 420–440 ppm, magnesium 1260–1350 ppm. PAR 100–250 / 200–300 cited. Care level moderate.

additional.name: AquariumDomain – Blue and Green Sympodium Species Profile
additional.notes: Cross-checked native range (Indo-Pacific), water parameters (temperature 72–80°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.023–1.025, dKH 8–12), flow (moderate to strong), placement (middle to high), care level (easy). Note: listed family as Zoanthidae — overridden by Wikipedia/LiveAquaria consensus of Xeniidae.

additional.name: Wikipedia – Sympodium (coral)
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy confirmation: Family Xeniidae, Order Malacalcyonacea (updated classification), Class Octocorallia. 10 recognized species in genus. Not used for husbandry parameters.

additional.name: Reef Chasers – Sympodium Coral Care Guide
additional.notes: Confirmed peaceful temperament, medium to strong flow preference, photosynthetic nutrition, easy propagation. Lacked specific parameter values.
